topic: Trump's threat to bomb Iran for one year contradicts WSJ report that Iran asked to continue negotiations
tweet1: Trump posted today that 1,000 missiles are 'locked and loaded' to decimate Iran for a full year if they try to kill him—hours after WSJ reported Iran told the U.S. it was a mistake to shoot ships and wants to keep negotiating.
tweet2: @FaytuksNetwork cited a US official confirming Iran recently said attacking commercial ships was an error and both sides should continue talks. That's not how a regime planning assassination behaves. Someone's lying or being lied to.
tweet3: @DropSiteNews reports Trump claims orders have 'already been given' to destroy Iran for 'one year.' But a sitting president can't pre-authorize military action contingent on his own death—that's not how command authority works under the Constitution.
tweet4: This isn't strategy. It's performance designed to keep Iran negotiations collapsed while Trump looks tough. If WSJ's source is real, the White House is ignoring an off-ramp. Who benefits from that?
